body: PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSS) projects palm oil prices to remain stable in 2025, ranging from 4,000 to 5,000 Malaysian ringgit per tonne, driven by government's B40 biodiesel programme and weather effects. The company reported a 7% increase in profit to IDR 955 billion in Q3 2024 compared to the same period last year, and plans to maintain its performance through development of terms management and continued savings on fertilizers.
title: Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera predicts stable palm oil prices in 2025, ranging from 4,000 to 5,000 Malaysian ringgit per tonne, driven by biodiesel demand and weather factors.

body: The Indonesian Stock Exchange has flagged unusual market activity in three issuers - PT Satria Antaran Prima Tbk (SAPX), PT Sunson Textile Manufacture Tbk (SSTM), and PT Trust Finance Indonesia Tbk (TRUS) - due to unusual price movements, with SAPX's share price plummeting 45% and SSTM's surging over 100% in a week. As of the latest trading session, SAPX's share price dropped 25% to IDR 1,020, SSTM's fell 13.43% to IDR 310, and TRUS's rose 15.54% to IDR 1,115.
title: Indonesian Stock Exchange Monitors Three Issuers Due to Unusual Price Movements

body: PT Golden Flower Tbk (GFL) shares surged 25% to IDR 1,855 per share, driven by a strong Q3 2024 revenue growth of 87.64% to IDR 103.45 billion, with trading volume reaching 375,800 shares worth IDR 545 million. Meanwhile, PT Perdana Bangun Pusaka Tbk (KONI) shares also rose 25% to IDR 1,455 per share, with a trading volume of 15,700 shares worth IDR 23.59 million.
title: PT Golden Flower Tbk shares surge 25% to top gainers list on strong Q3 revenue growth.

body: Garibaldi Thohir increased his stake in PT Provident Investasi Bersama Tbk (unlisted) to 17.48% from 15.36% through a transaction last week, resulting in a share price correction of IDR 2 to IDR 360 per share. As of November 30, 2024, the shareholder structure of PT Provident Investasi Bersama Tbk consists of PT Provident Capital with 55.67%, PT Saratoga Sentra with 8.97%, and others, with a free float of 1.34 billion shares or 8.53%.
title: Garibaldi Thohir increases stake in PT Provident Investasi Bersama to 17.48% through recent transaction.

body: Sight Investment Company Pte Limited increased its stake in PT Siloam International Hospitals Tbk (SILO) to 63.44% by acquiring 1.04 billion shares, while CGS International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd acquired 152.66 million shares of PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI), raising its stake to 15.13%. Other notable transactions include PT Adaro Energy Indonesia Tbk (ADRO) selling 146.12 million shares of PT Adaro Andalan Indonesia Tbk (AADI), and PT Kalbe Farma Tbk (KLBF) selling 3.75 million shares of PT Enseval Putera Megatrading Tbk (EPMT).
title: Foreign investors and local companies make significant changes to their stakes in various Indonesian companies across healthcare, media, finance, and mining sectors.

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index declined 0.79% or 57.99 points to IDR 7,324.79 last week, driven by high net selling volume from foreign investors, who recorded a net sell of IDR 2.7 trillion (USD 169.02 million). Meanwhile, PT Dian Swastatika Sentosa Tbk (DSSA) shares led the gainers, rising 6.87%, while PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (CAP) shares were among the top losers, falling 10.37%.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Falls 0.79% Last Week Due to High Net Selling by Foreign Investors

body: PT Adaro Strategic Investments acquired 3.20 billion shares of PT Adaro Andalan Indonesia Tbk (ADRO), increasing its stake to 24.05%, while PT Adaro Energy Indonesia Tbk (ADEI) reduced its shareholding from 90% to 11.18%. Meanwhile, various companies and investors made notable transactions, including PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk, PT Superior Prima Sukses Tbk, PT Surya Citra Media Tbk, PT Sinar Mas Cakrawala, PT Fap Agri Tbk, and PT Bumi Resources Tbk, resulting in changes to their ownership stakes.
title: Adaro Strategic Investments becomes new shareholder of PT Adaro Indonesia with 24.05% stake, among other notable transactions in Indonesian stock market.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 986.55 million shares, with significant sales in the banking sector, including PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BBRI), PT Bank Mandiri (BMRI), and PT Bank Neo Commerce (BBYB), as well as in digital company PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia (GOTO). Meanwhile, they purchased shares in energy and infrastructure companies, such as PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur (ASTR), PT Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison (ISAT), and PT Perusahaan Gas Negara (PGAS).
title: Foreign investors sell 3.3 billion shares, dominate banking sector sales, and buy into energy and telecom companies in Indonesia.

body: PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur TBK (ASTR) topped the net foreign list with a net foreign amount of 165.88 million shares, amidst price fluctuations, with its share price climbing 27.37% over the past month. Meanwhile, PT Indosat TBK (ISAT) shares rose to the runner-up position with a net foreign amount of 28.28 million shares, and its share price grew 19.54% this month, closing at IDR2,630 per share.
title: PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tops Net Foreign List, PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Exits Top 10

body: PT Visi Media Asia Tbk (VIVA) acquired 17.64 billion shares of PT Intermedia Capital Tbk, making it a new major stakeholder with a 45% stake, while foreign investor CGS International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd increased its stake in PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI) to 14.5%. Meanwhile, PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi Tbk (Elang) expanded its stake in PT Surya Citra Media Tbk, and PT Tancorp Investama Mulia increased its stake in PT Superior Prima Sukses Tbk, among other notable transactions.
title: Multiple Indonesian companies see changes in ownership stakes through recent share transactions.

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index (JCI) closed down 0.94% or 69.45 points to 7,324.79, with all sectoral indices declining, led by transportation (-1.29%) and health (-0.90%). Foreign investors recorded a net sale of IDR 146.02 billion, with a total transaction value of IDR 12.08 trillion and a volume of 183.37 million lots traded on the stock exchange.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Closes Down 0.94% Amid Broad Sectoral Declines

body: Amir Sunarko acquired 5.61% of PT SLJ Global Tbk's shares, while Anderson Bay Pte Ltd and CGS International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd increased their stakes in PT Exploitasi Energi Indonesia Tbk and PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk, respectively. Meanwhile, PT Shima Global Kapital released 45 million shares of PT Energi Mega Persada Tbk, and PT Sinar Mas Cakrawala reduced its stake in PT Sinar Mas Multiartha Tbk, among other notable transactions.
title: Investors Make Significant Moves in Indonesian Stock Market with Notable Purchases and Sales of Shares in Various Companies

body: PT Indofarma Tbk (INA) plans to divest over 50% of its assets to settle its liabilities, which stood at IDR 1.73 trillion as of Q3 2024, with short-term liabilities exceeding current assets by sixfold. The company aims to use the proceeds from the divestment to address its capital deficiency of IDR 970.63 billion, pay creditors, and fund employee rightsizing and working capital.
title: Indofarma plans to divest over 50% of its assets to settle liabilities and address capital deficiency.

body: CBDK is set to offer 566.89 million shares in its IPO, priced between IDR 3,000 and IDR 4,060 each, potentially raising IDR 2.3 trillion in fresh funds at the highest price level. The company, which generated 99% of its total net revenue from the real estate sector in the first half of 2024, has total assets of IDR 18.16 trillion and equity of IDR 8.14 trillion as of June 30, 2024.
title: CBDK to Raise Up to IDR 2.3 Trillion in IPO with 566.89 Million Shares Offered

body: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) is optimistic about surpassing its 2024 gold production target of 35,000 troy ounces, with 45,336 troy ounces already produced in the first nine months, driven by its second gold refining facility reaching full capacity. The company expects gold production to reach over 70,000 troy ounces in 2025 and over 85,000 troy ounces in 2026, with underground mineral reserves mining at Proboya expected to commence soon.
title: Bumi Resources Minerals Expects Gold Production to Surpass 55,000 Troy Ounces This Year
